india_trends.title: World’s largest container carrier plans route avoiding Hormuz
india_trends.summary: The world’s largest container shipping company, MSC Mediterranean Shipping Company, is launching a new Europe–Middle East service that avoids the Strait of Hormuz by combining sea, road, and feeder ship routes.

india_trends.title: FPIs pull out ₹13,944 crore in holiday-shortened week, equities hit hardest
india_trends.summary: While outflows showed signs of moderation towards the end of the week, global headwinds, rising crude prices, and geopolitical tensions continued to weigh on sentiment

india_trends.title: Specialised investment funds see rapid growth, attract beyond top 30 cities
india_trends.summary: AUM has more than doubled to ₹10,620 crore, with fund houses launching new offerings and eyeing ₹1 lakh crore in assets over the next three years, despite distribution challenges

india_trends.title: India notifies 100% foreign investment in insurance sector, LIC limit stays at 20%
india_trends.summary: The central government has notified 100% Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) in insurance companies under the automatic route, allowing foreign investors to fully invest in the sector without prior approvals, according to a report by ET Now citing agencies.

india_trends.title: India eases HVDC localisation rules, sets timeline to reach 60% local content by 2035
india_trends.summary: The Ministry of Power has updated its 'Make in India' rules for the power sector. A new plan will gradually increase local content in HVDC substations. This aims for 60 percent Indian-made components by 2035. The changes provide a transition for domestic industries to grow their capacity.

india_trends.title: CDSL Q4 Results: Cons PAT declines 20% to Rs 80 crore, Rs 12.75/share dividend declared
india_trends.summary: CDSL Q4 Results: Central Depository Services Limited reported a mixed Q4 FY26 performance, with revenue growing 17.1% to Rs 262.85 crore, but net profit declining 20% to Rs 80.22 crore. The company surpassed 18 crore demat accounts, highlighting growing investor trust despite profitability pressures

india_trends.title: FII outflows not driven by lack of AI and high taxes, says Shankar Sharma. Here’s why - The Economic Times
india_trends.summary: FII outflows not driven by lack of AI and high taxes, says Shankar Sharma. Here’s why    The Economic Times

india_trends.title: FII outflows not driven by lack of AI and high taxes, says Shankar Sharma. Here’s why
india_trends.summary: Foreign investors are not avoiding India due to AI exposure or tax issues, according to investor Shankar Sharma. Data shows markets with little AI have outperformed. India's own past performance contradicts the AI dependency narrative. FII flows and taxation show a weak link to market direction. Glo

india_trends.title: Air India CEO flags flight cuts as jet fuel spike makes routes unviable
india_trends.summary: Air India is reducing flights due to soaring jet fuel costs and airspace issues. International operations are becoming unprofitable. Further schedule trims are planned for June and July. Domestic flights are also affected but less severely. The airline is increasing fares and adding fuel surcharges.

india_trends.title: Kotak Mahindra Bank Q4 Results: PAT jumps 13% to Rs 4,026.55 crore, NII rises 8%
india_trends.summary: Kotak Mahindra Bank Q4 Results: Kotak Mahindra Bank on Saturday reported a net profit of Rs 4,026.55 crore for the March quarter of FY26, marking a jump of 13.3% from Rs 3,552 crore posted in the corresponding quarter of the previous financial year.

india_trends.title: IFSCA in talks with RBI over foreign asset reporting rule for GIFT City funds
india_trends.summary: The move follows an RBI FAQ that set out mandatory FLA filing requirements and raised questions over the regulatory classification of entities in the GIFT IFSC, prompting industry concerns over potential compliance overlap.

india_trends.title: Rohit Jain appointed RBI deputy governor for three-year term
india_trends.summary: Rohit Jain has been appointed as the new deputy governor of the Reserve Bank of India. His term will begin on May 3 and last for three years. Jain currently serves as an executive director at the central bank. He takes over from T Rabi Sankar, who recently retired.

india_trends.title: 'China will determine the Global Trading Order' - ET Titan Talks | Dr Ram Charan | Teaser
Nifty stays range bound as volatility rises; breakout awaited
india_trends.summary: Nifty 50 traded within a narrow range, closing slightly higher amid rising volatility and weak momentum indicators. Markets remain in consolidation, with key resistance and support levels intact, while sector rotation suggests selective opportunities despite the absence of strong directional trends.

india_trends.title: Nifty stays range bound as volatility rises; breakout awaited - The Economic Times
india_trends.summary: Nifty stays range bound as volatility rises; breakout awaited    The Economic Times

india_trends.title: India and Ecuador exploring preferential trade deal to boost economic ties
india_trends.summary: India and Ecuador are strengthening ties. Discussions focused on a preferential trade agreement. Cooperation will expand in healthcare, agriculture, and digital technology. Ecuador will join the International Solar Alliance and International Big Cat Alliance. India will provide grant assistance for

india_trends.title: Net leasing of office spaces in NCR down 60 pc in Jan-Mar to 1.5 mn sq ft: JLL
india_trends.summary: Delhi-NCR's office market experienced a subdued first quarter with net leasing falling 60% to 1.5 million sq ft, primarily due to reduced new supply. Despite this dip, JLL India maintains that long-term fundamentals remain strong, with leasing activities anticipated to increase in the coming quarter

india_trends.title: Largecap IT stocks as a value play? BNP Paribas’ Kumar Rakesh issues a reality check
india_trends.summary: BNP Paribas analyst Kumar Rakesh cautions that weak FY27 guidance, AI-led disruption, and stalled client spending challenge the IT sector’s “value” narrative. While buybacks and dividends provide support, earnings risks remain elevated, making selective stock picking crucial for investors.

india_trends.title: Axis Bank opens its first digital locker-focussed branch
india_trends.summary: Axis Bank has opened its first digital locker branch in New Delhi. This new facility aims to provide advanced security and customer experience through automated locker services. The bank is responding to a growing demand for safe deposit lockers, especially in urban areas. Industry estimates predict

india_trends.title: TVS Motor Company total sales up 7 pc at 4,73,970 units in April
india_trends.summary: TVS Motor Company announced strong sales figures for April 2026. Total sales saw a 7 percent increase. Two-wheeler sales also grew, driven by a significant jump in scooter sales. Electric vehicle sales and three-wheeler sales registered substantial growth. International business also contributed to

world_trends.title: This Japanese toilet maker seeking ‘the perfect flush’ is moonlighting as a hot AI play
world_trends.summary: Toto is “aiming to achieve a toilet that never needs cleaning.” It’s also supplying electrostatic chucks — a semiconductor-manufacturing component critical to the memory-chip boom.

world_trends.title: Japan just put a ‘Band-Aid’ on the yen. Why high oil prices could soon rip it off.
world_trends.summary: Concerns about high oil prices are adding to inflation fears in Japan, as the BOJ just moved to rescue the yen from a roughly 40-year low.

world_trends.title: ‘We’re worried the honey pot will run dry’: Does the Trump administration use my Social Security to fund federal programs?
world_trends.summary: There are many suggestions floating around Washington on how to fix the program’s funding issues.
